inbox/javascript_dom_screenshot.md.description: This document contains a screenshot of a web browser's console or developer tools displaying a detailed JavaScript object representing the document object model (DOM) of a webpage. The text is formatted with different colors to distinguish between keys, values, and data types, with keys in white, strings in light blue, null values in light purple, and other values in various colors. The structure is hierarchical, with nested objects and arrays indicated by curly braces and square brackets respectively. The purpose of this document is to provide a visual representation of the DOM for debugging or educational purposes.

inbox/ibm_technology_board_connections.p.title: IBM Technology Board Connections
inbox/ibm_technology_board_connections.p.alt_text:
This image is a network diagram showing the board connections of IBM Technology with various companies. The diagram includes nodes representing companies like IBM, Apple, Johnson & Johnson, Chevron, and others, connected by lines indicating board member connections. Key details include:
- IBM has 4 board connections.
- Notable Power Brokers: Al Gore, connects Apple and Honeywell International through J&J; Indra Nooyi, connects PepsiCo to Chevron.
- Companies like Apple, Johnson & Johnson, Chevron, and others are shown with their connections.
- The nodes are color-coded: IBM is blue, Apple is purple, Johnson & Johnson is green, Chevron is yellow, and others are grey.
- Text annotations provide additional information about the connections, such as 'J&J legacy with healthcare expertise' and 'Board Director (APPL, Board Chair IBM)'. 
- The background is white, and the lines connecting the nodes are grey.

inbox/nyt_top_stories_api_spec.yaml.description: This document is a Swagger specification for the New York Times Top Stories API. It outlines the structure and endpoints for accessing articles from various sections of the NYT website. The API allows users to retrieve an array of articles from specified sections like arts, business, health, etc., or from the homepage. The document includes details on how to use the API, including example calls, security requirements (API key), and the structure of the response which includes article metadata like title, abstract, URL, and multimedia content.

inbox/edit_plugin_configuration_patreon_.description: The document contains a detailed description of a user interface screenshot for editing plugin configuration. The purpose of this document is to provide a visual guide or reference for configuring a plugin, specifically for a Patreon URL.

inbox/january_2025_financial_transaction.description: This document contains financial transaction data for January 2025, including dates, transaction descriptions, amounts, and categories. It serves as a record of financial activities for the month, useful for budgeting, financial analysis, and tracking expenses and income.

inbox/ollama_chat_interface.py.description: This document is a Python module named 'ollama_chat_interface_renamed.py' that implements an interface to the Ollama API for streaming chat responses. It supports model selection, multi-turn conversations, image analysis, and file handling with streaming responses, compatible with both local and cloud Ollama instances. The module includes classes and methods for initializing the client, fetching available models, listing models with sorting and pagination, creating test images, encoding files and images to base64, and formatting messages with attachments for the API. It also handles error checking for file sizes and supported types.

inbox/federal_spending_dashboard_2022.ht.description: This HTML document represents a Federal Spending Dashboard for the year 2022, focusing on federal receipts and expenditures by state. It includes interactive elements such as a theme toggle, sorting options, and a chart type toggle, suggesting it is designed for user interaction and data visualization. The document uses modern UI libraries like Bootstrap Icons, Chart.js, and D3.js for styling and data representation. The purpose of this document is to provide an interactive platform for users to explore and analyze federal spending data across different states.

inbox/google_cloud_run_metrics_camina_ol.description: The document provides a detailed view of the Google Cloud Platform interface for the 'camina-ollama' Cloud Run service under the 'Actually Useful AI' project. It includes the service's URL, region, and minimum instance count. The document focuses on the 'METRICS' tab, displaying predefined metrics with no errors reported. It features two graphs: one for request count showing a spike at 4:00 PM with 3 requests, and another for request latencies peaking at 0.02s. The document also mentions a Cloud Shell terminal and annotations. The purpose of this document is to provide an overview of the service's performance metrics and operational status.
